name: spherical-ml-biodiversity — what spherical ML is, why it matters, and a worked replication on Copernicus data tied to biodiversity
description: Pedagogical and supporting-evidence repository operationalising the spherical-ML claim from dggs-biodiversity-bias. Six notebooks: (01) the failure mode of flat CNNs on global lat-lon rasters, with the same physical 10° spherical cap distorting differently at 0/40/70°N; (02) rotation equivariance — what spherical CNNs preserve and flat CNNs don't, with 50.6% pixel disagreement when treating rotation as translation and 100% → 55% peak-response collapse of an equator-cap matched filter from 0°N to 80°N; (03) why HEALPix is the right substrate — equal-area cells + iso-latitude rings + NESTED bit-shift hierarchical refinement + graph structure for DeepSphere-style graph CNNs; (04) head-to-head detection comparison on synthetic global SST, where a flat lat-lon matched-filter baseline collapses from 1.000 accuracy at 0–20° to 0.500 at 70–80° while a HEALPix sphere-harmonic matched filter (band-pass kernel via aₗₘ → aₗₘ · fₗ · bₗ) holds at 1.000 across all four test latitude bands; (05) a real marine heatwave — the 2011 Western Australian Ningaloo Niño on NOAA OISST v2.1 aggregated to HEALPix-NESTED nside=128, with 24.7% of GBIF marine biodiversity occurrences from 2011 in the region falling on MHW-exposed cells, connecting the failure-mode arc to documented biodiversity impacts (Wernberg et al. 2016 kelp regime shift); (06) cross-discipline transfer on the spherical substrate — the conceptual case that sphere-aware models trained in one discipline transfer to others on the same HEALPix substrate. The DeepSphere atmospheric-river replication and AR-biodiversity-Europe follow-up live in the separate annefou/deepsphere-ar-replication repository (paper-rooted FORRT chain). This is the spherical-ML side of the ESA GRID4EARTH case for Ellipsoidal HEALPix as a Common DGGS bridging Copernicus EO and Destination Earth.
